components.name: App Header
elements: Messenger Icon (chat bubble with '9+' notification badge)

components.name: Post Creation Module
elements: Text Input Field ('What's on your mind?')

elements.name: Story Card (multiple instances)
content: Profile Picture (circular, some with blue border)

components.name: Feed Post
elements: Post Media (large image of a woman with children and money)
Overlay Profile Picture (small, circular, of the post author on the media)

components.name: Bottom Navigation Bar
elements: Friends Tab (icon: two people, text: 'Friends', with '1' notification badge)
Notifications Tab (icon: bell, text: 'Notifications', with '2' notification badge)

layout_structure:
The screen features a top-down hierarchical layout:
1.  A fixed Status Bar at the very top.
2.  A fixed App Header below the status bar for global navigation and branding.
3.  A Post Creation Module acting as a quick entry point for user-generated content.
4.  A horizontally scrollable Stories Section presenting ephemeral content.
5.  A vertically scrollable main content area for the user Feed, displaying individual posts in a card-like format.
6.  A fixed Bottom Navigation Bar for primary app navigation.
Elements are generally aligned to a vertical grid, with consistent padding and margins separating distinct sections and components. Stories are arranged in a horizontal grid within their section.

typography.font_family: Sans-serif (appears to be a system default or custom font like Roboto/Helvetica Neue)

spacing_and_density.vertical_spacing: Generous spacing between major sections (header, post creation, stories, feed). Standard spacing between elements within a post.
spacing_and_density.horizontal_spacing: Compact within the app header and bottom navigation. Stories are tightly packed horizontally.
spacing_and_density.padding: Consistent padding around content blocks (e.g., post creation module, individual posts) and within interactive elements. Good use of whitespace for readability.

accessibility_observations: High color contrast for most text and interactive elements (dark text/blue on white background) generally aids readability.
Icons are standard and generally recognizable, enhancing intuitive navigation.
Notification badges are clearly visible due to contrasting red color and white text.
Tap targets for main interactive elements (buttons, navigation tabs, story cards) appear sufficiently large.
Potential for low contrast on text overlays on user-generated story/post images, which might affect readability for some users.
The small privacy icon in the post header might be difficult to discern or tap for users with fine motor skill challenges or visual impairments.
notable_patterns: Standard social media Home Feed layout (header, story carousel, main feed).
Top App Bar for global actions and branding.
Persistent Bottom Navigation Bar for primary app navigation.
Horizontal Story Carousel for ephemeral content display.
Card-based UI for organizing feed posts and stories.
Notification Badges for indicating unread counts.
'What's on your mind?' text input as a common pattern for user content creation.
Circular Profile Pictures for user identification.
